title: Red Bull clever tactics as Lewis Hamilton crash proves costly
summary:
ed Bull used clever tactics to get Max Verstappen onto the front row of the grid for the Belgian Grand Prix, although there was no stopping championship leader Kimi Antonelli who claimed pole in Spa-Francorchamps.

With Isack Hadjar having a 30-place grid penalty, the Frenchman gave Verstappen a huge tow, making a massive difference come the final part of qualifying. Lando Norris secured third but starts from 13th due to a grid penalty, while Lewis Hamilton's car failed to feel the same after a big accident in third practice.

Join RacingNews365's Nick Golding and Samuel Coop as they dissect Belgian GP Qualifying!
